title: What car characteristics influence Fuel Efficiency? A Data Visualization
text:
This report investigates with data visualizations which characteristics influence fuel efficiency of cars on the American market between 1984 and 2014. The variables analysed are: type of gear, number of gears, number of cylinders and brand. The data is from the US Department of Energy and available here: https://www.fueleconomy.gov/feg/download.shtml.

1 Fuel Efficiency over Time








Figure 1: Fuel efficiency over Time






in 1985 the average fuel consumption of an American car was 14.3 liters in the city and 13.3 liters on the highway
in 2014 the average fuel consumption was 12.4 liters in the city and 8.9 liters on the highway
although both measurements decreased overtime, fuel efficiency increased higher on the highway
between 1985 and 2006 fuel consumption stayed on a similar level
only since 2006 cars on the American market got more efficient



2 Automatic Gear








Figure 2: Relationship between the Share of Automatic Gear and Fuel Efficiency per Year






automatic gearboxes were a popular development in the American car market
by the mid-1980s, more than half of American cars had automatic gears
by 2014, the share of automatic gearboxes increased to more than four fitht
Figure 3 shows a relationship between a higher share of automatic gearboxes and fuel efficiency: the higher the share of automatic gearboxes the higher is the average fuel efficiency
however this relationship is non-linear and intensifies in the late 2000s



3 Number of Gears








Figure 3: Relationship between the Number of Gears and Fuel Efficiency













Figure 4: Mean and Predominant Number of Gears, Time and Fuel Efficiency






more gears are associated with better fuel efficiency
since 2009 six gears predominate the American market



4 Number of Cylinders








Figure 5: Relationship between the Number of Cylinders and Fuel Efficiency






with each additional cylinder fuel efficiency decreases
except for 12 cylinders in the city and 12 and 10 cylinders on the highway



5 Brand








Figure 6: Relationship between Fuel Efficiency in the City and on the Highway for the Top 12 Brands






brands differ highly in the distribution of their models
Volvo and Subarus fuel efficiency is very similar between models
brands like Dodge and Chevrolet have models with a wide range of fuel consumptions



6 Conclusion
Key Findings

fuel efficiency increased since the late 2000s
a higher share of automatic gears is associated with better fuel efficiency
cars with a higher number of gears have in general a better fuel efficiency
a lower number of cylinders is associated with a higher fuel efficiency

Limitations

only descriptive analysis is used
to confirm results a technical analysis is needed



7 Data Source
Fuel economy data is provided by the U.S. Department of Energy:
https://www.fueleconomy.gov/feg/download.shtml
